meta.name: SSS-8 Somatic Symptom Scale
meta.description: The SSS-8 is a brief 8-item self-report measure for assessing somatic symptom burden, abbreviated from the PHQ-15 by Gierk et al. It covers gastrointestinal, pain, fatigue, and cardiopulmonary domains.
meta.author: Gierk et al. (2014)
meta.reference: Gierk B, Kohlmann S, Kroenke K, et al. The Somatic Symptom Scale-8 (SSS-8): A brief measure of somatic symptom burden. JAMA Intern Med. 2014;174(3):399-407.
meta.instruction: During the past 7 days, how much have you been bothered by each of the following symptoms?

questions.text: Pain in your arms, legs, or joints (knees, hips, etc.)
Chest pain or shortness of breath
Feeling tired or having low energy
Trouble falling asleep or staying asleep

ranges.label: No/Minimal Somatic Symptoms
ranges.description: Your somatic symptom burden is very low, falling within the normal range for the general population. You report minimal or no bothersome physical symptoms across gastrointestinal, pain, cardiopulmonary, and fatigue domains. This suggests good overall physical comfort and well-being.
suggestions: Continue maintaining the healthy lifestyle habits that support your physical well-being
Occasional minor physical discomfort is a normal fluctuation and not a cause for concern
Regular exercise, balanced nutrition, and adequate sleep help prevent somatic symptom development

ranges.label: Mild Somatic Symptoms
ranges.description: You report mild somatic symptoms that may be related to everyday stress, lifestyle factors, or minor physical conditions. These symptoms are noticeable but not significantly disabling. Attention to self-care and monitoring for changes is advisable.
suggestions: Monitor your symptoms over time and note any patterns related to stress, diet, sleep, or activity levels
Regular physical exercise and structured relaxation techniques such as deep breathing may help reduce symptom burden
Ensure you are maintaining adequate sleep, hydration, and balanced nutrition as basic self-care measures
If symptoms persist beyond two weeks or worsen, consider scheduling an appointment with your primary care physician

ranges.label: Moderate Somatic Symptoms
ranges.description: Your somatic symptom burden is moderate and may be affecting your daily functioning, work performance, or quality of life. Multiple physical symptoms across different body systems suggest a pattern that warrants medical attention and exploration of contributing factors.
suggestions: Consider scheduling a medical appointment for a thorough physical examination to evaluate your symptoms
Understand the mind-body connection: psychological stress frequently manifests as physical symptoms and vice versa
Regular physical exercise and consistent sleep schedules can help reduce somatic symptom burden significantly
Explore whether current life stressors, anxiety, or mood changes may be contributing to your physical symptoms
Consider psychological counseling or stress management therapy if physical evaluations do not fully explain your symptoms

ranges.label: High Somatic Symptoms
ranges.description: Your somatic symptom burden is high and may be significantly affecting your daily activities, work capacity, and overall quality of life. You are experiencing substantial physical discomfort across multiple symptom domains. A comprehensive evaluation addressing both physical and psychological factors is recommended.
suggestions: A comprehensive medical examination is recommended to identify any treatable physical conditions contributing to your symptoms
A concurrent mental health evaluation can help identify psychological factors that may be amplifying somatic symptoms
Learn about somatic symptom disorder and how the mind-body connection influences physical symptom perception and severity
An integrated treatment approach addressing both physical and psychological aspects simultaneously tends to be most effective
Track your symptoms daily to identify triggers, patterns, and fluctuations that can inform your treatment plan

ranges.label: Very High Somatic Symptoms
ranges.description: You report a very high somatic symptom burden indicating severe physical discomfort across multiple body systems. This level of symptom severity likely causes substantial impairment in daily functioning, work, and social activities. A comprehensive psychosomatic evaluation is strongly recommended.
suggestions: Please see a healthcare provider soon for a thorough physical examination to rule out or treat any underlying medical conditions
A psychiatric or psychological consultation is also strongly recommended to evaluate stress-related or somatoform contributions
Severe somatic symptoms often require a comprehensive, multidisciplinary treatment plan integrating medical and psychological care
Do not dismiss or ignore the significant impact these symptoms are having on your quality of life; effective treatments are available

gastrointestinal.label: Low
gastrointestinal.description: Minimal gastrointestinal symptoms reported. Your digestive system appears to be functioning comfortably without significant stomach or bowel complaints.

gastrointestinal.label: Moderate
gastrointestinal.description: Some gastrointestinal discomfort is present, including stomach or bowel problems that may be stress-related or dietary in nature. Monitor for persistent or worsening patterns.

gastrointestinal.label: High
gastrointestinal.description: Notable gastrointestinal symptoms are reported. Persistent stomach or bowel problems at this level warrant medical evaluation to assess underlying causes and appropriate management.

pain.label: Low
pain.description: Minimal pain symptoms across back, limbs, joints, and head. Your musculoskeletal and headache symptom burden is very low and within normal range.

pain.label: Moderate
pain.description: Moderate pain symptoms including back pain, joint discomfort, or headaches that may affect daily comfort and activity levels. Consider ergonomic adjustments and regular physical activity.

pain.label: High
pain.description: Notable pain symptoms reported across multiple areas. This level of musculoskeletal pain and headaches may significantly impact daily activities. Medical evaluation and pain management strategies are recommended.

cardiopulmonary.label: Low
cardiopulmonary.description: Minimal cardiopulmonary symptoms reported. You are not experiencing significant chest pain, shortness of breath, or dizziness at this time.

cardiopulmonary.label: Moderate
cardiopulmonary.description: Some cardiopulmonary discomfort including chest pain, breathing difficulties, or dizziness is present. While these may be benign, monitoring and medical evaluation are advisable if persistent.

cardiopulmonary.label: High
cardiopulmonary.description: Notable cardiopulmonary symptoms are reported including significant chest pain, shortness of breath, or dizziness. Medical evaluation is recommended to rule out cardiac or pulmonary conditions.

fatigue.label: Low
fatigue.description: Minimal fatigue reported. Your energy levels and sleep quality appear to be adequate for daily functioning and responsibilities.

fatigue.label: Moderate
fatigue.description: Moderate fatigue and some sleep difficulties are present, potentially affecting your daytime energy, concentration, and overall productivity. Attention to sleep hygiene may be helpful.

fatigue.label: High
fatigue.description: Notable fatigue and significant sleep problems are reported. This level of tiredness and sleep disruption may substantially impair daily functioning. Evaluation of sleep habits, underlying conditions, and stress factors is recommended.
